title: Still leaving your home unwatched? These 5 indoor security cameras help you keep an eye on everything
snippet: All of these security cameras offer 360-degree of monitoring. In addition to this, these cameras also offer support for night vision technology.

title: Semicon 2.0: Govt to match VC funding in chip design, pick equity
snippet: Under the scheme unveiled on Wednesday, the government will match private investments in eligible startups in return for equity to reduce funding gaps in one of the country's most capital-intensive technology sectors.

title: BGMI 4.5 update: Naruto themed maps, Spider-Man mode, Ferrari cars and everything new
snippet: KRAFTON has launched BGMI 4.5, introducing a Naruto-themed gameplay mode with new locations and abilities, alongside a Spider-Man crossover, licensed Ferrari vehicles, and anniversary rewards.&nbsp;

title: Vivo T5 Lite 44W 5G in India with 120Hz display, 6,500mAh battery:  Check price and specs
snippet: The Vivo T5 Lite 44W 5G features a 6.74-inch HD+ display, MediaTek Dimensity 6300 processor, and a 6,500mAh battery.&nbsp;

title: Apple reveals businesses that won't be allowed to advertise on Apple Maps
snippet: Apple plans to introduce ads on Apple Maps, restricting certain businesses from advertising.

title: Microsoft trains sales team to talk down OpenAI, Anthropic AI models, pitches itself as 'full AI platform': Report
snippet: Microsoft is training sales teams to compete against Anthropic and OpenAI by emphasizing its superior offerings in AI.&nbsp;

title: Google to allow third-party app stores inside Play Store from next week after Epic lawsuit
snippet: Google will permit third-party app stores on its Play Store in the US starting next week, ending its legal battle with Epic Games.&nbsp;

title: Cabinet approves  ₹1.9 tn push for chips, mobiles to deepen local manufacturing
snippet: At the centre of the package is Semicon 2.0, a  ₹1.27 trillion programme aimed at helping Indian firms design chips, generate patents. The cabinet also cleared a  ₹62,500 crore mobile phone manufacturing scheme that rewards companies for sourcing more components locally

title: Motorola Edge 70 Max launched in India with Snapdragon 8 Gen 5, 7,100mAh battery: Check price, specs and launch offers
snippet: Motorola Edge 70 Max has launched in India with the Snapdragon 8 Gen 5 chipset, a 7,100mAh battery, 2K AMOLED display, 90W fast charging and up to 12GB RAM. Check price, offers and full specifications.

title: OpenAI's first AI device may know your emails, control your home and talk like a human
snippet: OpenAI is developing a portable AI-powered smart speaker as its first consumer hardware product, aimed to help users with smart-home control, music, and more.&nbsp;

title: Chinese AI model takes US tech industry by surprise with abilities rivalling Claude, ChatGPT
snippet: The newest Kimi K3 model from Beijing-based startup Moonshot, run by a Pink Floyd-loving entrepreneur who earned his doctorate in Pittsburgh, appears to be catching up to the best versions of Anthropic's Claude and OpenAI's ChatGPT.

title: Meta, Anthropic in talks for potential $10 billion compute lease deal
snippet: Such a deal would help Meta diversify beyond advertising by generating ​revenue from its infrastructure and competing with neocloud firms such as CoreWeave and Nebius, ​as growing adoption of advanced AI tools boosts the need for computing capacity.

title: Governance is the new moat in AI era: ServiceNow Top executive
snippet: Kulmeet Bawa, managing director for India and SAARC at ServiceNow said the company’s next phase of growth lies in becoming the “AI control tower” for enterprises by orchestrating AI workflows across multiple software systems rather than competing directly with foundation model providers.

Additionally, the state has maintained its top position by securing a 24% share in the nation's overall export sector. title: Apple in early settlement talks with US DOJ over antitrust case, Bloomberg News reports
snippet: Apple is reportedly in early settlement discussions with the U.S. Department of Justice regarding the ongoing antitrust case. This news, as reported by Bloomberg, suggests a potential resolution to the legal battle between the tech giant and the government over alleged anti-competitive practices.
